One of many tissues in the body that can tighten and relax to produce movement.
Either of the two organs in the chest with which people and animals breathe.
The middle joint of the leg that allows the leg to bend.
A large organ in the body that cleans the blood and produces bile.
One of the two bones at the top of your cheeks, just below your eyes.
The thick curved part at the back of the human leg between the knee and the ankle.
The front part of the leg between the knee and the ankle.
One of the parts of the body of a human or animal that is used for standing or walking.
The part of the body at the bottom of the leg on which a person or animal stands.
The rounded back part of the foot.
The muscular organ in the chest that pumps blood throughout the body.
An organ near the stomach that produces insulin and juices that help the body digest food.
The part in the middle of the arm where it bends.
The part of a person's leg above the knee.
The part of the body at the end of the arm which has fingers and a thumb.
The joint between the foot and the leg, or the thin part of the leg just above the foot.
Either of the two large muscles on the chest.
A thin, hard area that covers the upper side of the end of each finger and toe.
The line of bones down the centre of the back that provides support for the body.
Any of the hard pieces that make up the skeleton of a human or animal body.
The organ inside the body where food goes after it is swallowed and where it starts to be digested.
The organ inside the head that controls thought, memory, feelings, and activity.
Either of the two organs in the body that remove waste products from the blood and produce urine.
The inside part of your hand between your fingers and your wrist.
The natural outer layer that covers a person, animal, or fruit.
Either of the two long parts of the upper body that are attached to the shoulders and have the hands at the ends.

expression: despite
spanish_sources: without being influenced or prevented by something

expression: beyond
spanish_sources: further away in the distance than something

expression: below
spanish_sources: less than a particular amount or level:

spanish_sources: except; not including

expression: into
spanish_sources: towards the inside or middle of something and about to be contained, surrounded, or enclosed by it

expression: Hip
spanish_sources: The area of the body below the waist and above the thighs at either side of the body.

expression: meat and drink
spanish_sources: a source of pleasure

expression: during
spanish_sources: from the beginning to the end of a period of time

expression: before
spanish_sources: at the time that is earlier than the time of something else

expression: Blood
spanish_sources: The red liquid that flows through the bodies of people and animals.

expression: Waist
spanish_sources: The part of the body in humans between the ribs and the hips, usually the narrowest part of the torso.

expression: aboard
spanish_sources: on or onto a ship, aircraft, bus, or train

expression: Back
spanish_sources: The part of your body that is opposite to the front, from your neck to the top of your legs.

expression: cranny
spanish_sources: a small, narrow opening in something solid:

expression: in
spanish_sources: during a period of time

expression: Neck
spanish_sources: The part of the body that joins the head to the shoulders.

expression: pancreas
spanish_sources: an organ in the body that produces insulin (= a chemical substance that controls the amount of sugar in the blood) and substances that help to digest food so that it can be used by the body

expression: amid
spanish_sources: in the middle of or surrounded by

expression: Shoulder
spanish_sources: One of the two parts of the body at each side of the neck where the arm joins the body.

expression: behind
spanish_sources: at the back of someone or something

expression: Chest
spanish_sources: The upper front part of the body of a human or animal, between the neck and the stomach.

expression: beside
spanish_sources: at the side of, or next to, somebody or something

expression: act up
spanish_sources: If a person, especially a child, acts up, they behave badly.

expression: At sixes and sevens
spanish_sources: in a confused, badly organized, or difficult situation:

expression: considering
spanish_sources: after thinking about or taking into account

expression: illness
spanish_sources: the state of being ill:

expression: The pros and cons
spanish_sources: the advantages and disadvantages of something, especially something that you are considering doing:

expression: Nape
spanish_sources: The back of the neck.

expression: alive and well
spanish_sources: still full of energy and activity:

expression: On and on
spanish_sources: Continually

expression: Toe
spanish_sources: Any of the five separate parts at the end of the foot.

expression: between
spanish_sources: in the space that separates two places, people, or objects

expression: Hurt Hurt Hurt
spanish_sources: to cause pain or injury to yourself or someone else

expression: Face
spanish_sources: The front part of the head, where the eyes, nose, and mouth are.

expression: Chock-a-block
spanish_sources: If a place is chock-a-block, it is very full of people or things:

expression: Pupil
spanish_sources: The circular black area in the centre of the eye that standardizes the amount of light entering.

expression: Spick and span
spanish_sources: Very clean and tidy

expression: eat up
spanish_sources: to eat all of some food

expression: Breast
spanish_sources: Either of the two soft, rounded parts of the body that grow on the chest of a woman.

expression: Navel
spanish_sources: The small, round, hollow place or small lump in the middle of the stomach.

expression: Cut Cut Cut
spanish_sources: to break the surface of something using a sharp tool such as a knife

expression: Abdomen
spanish_sources: The lower part of a person's or animal's body, containing the stomach, bowels, and other organs.

expression: from
spanish_sources: used to show the place where someone or something starts

expression: Eyebrow
spanish_sources: The line of short hairs above each of your eyes.

expression: bright and early
spanish_sources: very early in the morning

expression: Shut Shut Shut
spanish_sources: to close something

expression: Cast Cast Cast
spanish_sources: to choose actors to play particular parts in a play, film, or show

expression: Bottom
spanish_sources: The part of your body that you sit on.

expression: Sooner or later
spanish_sources: used to say that you do not know exactly when something will happen, but you are certain that it will happen:

expression: Forearm
spanish_sources: The lower part of the arm, between the wrist and the elbow.

expression: like
spanish_sources: similar to or in the same way as

expression: Thumb
spanish_sources: The short, thick finger on the side of your hand that makes it possible to hold things easily.

expression: along
spanish_sources: from one end to or towards the other end of something

expression: for
spanish_sources: intended to be given to or used by; because of or for the reason of

expression: Cost Cost Cost
spanish_sources: if something costs an amount of money, you must pay that amount to buy or do it

expression: as
spanish_sources: in the role of; with the identity of

expression: HIther and thither
spanish_sources: in many directions:

expression: Buttock
spanish_sources: Either of the two round parts of the human body that you sit on.

expression: Belly
spanish_sources: The stomach or the front part of the body between the chest and the legs.

expression: Finger
spanish_sources: Any of the long, thin, separate parts of the hand, especially those that are not the thumb.

expression: chew
spanish_sources: to crush food into smaller, softer pieces with the teeth so that it can be swallowed:

expression: Nose
spanish_sources: The part of the face that sticks out above the mouth, used for breathing and smelling.

expression: be done and dusted
spanish_sources: to be complete and finished:

expression: Eyelash
spanish_sources: Any of the short hairs that grow along the edges of the eye.

expression: by
spanish_sources: used to show the person or thing that does something, or the means used

expression: following
spanish_sources: as a result of a particular event

expression: blow-up
spanish_sources: needing to be filled with air in order to be used

expression: Head
spanish_sources: The part of the body above the neck where the eyes, nose, mouth, ears, and brain are.

expression: Iris
spanish_sources: The coloured circular part of the eye that surrounds the black pupil.

expression: Stomach
spanish_sources: an organ in the body where food is digested

expression: intestine
spanish_sources: (either of the two parts of) a long tube through which food travels from the stomach and out of the body while it is being digested:

expression: Forehead
spanish_sources: The flat part of the face, above the eyes and below the hair.

expression: testicle
spanish_sources: either of the two round male sex organs that produce sperm and are contained in the scrotum (= bag of skin) below and behind the penis

expression: Now and then
spanish_sources: sometimes, but not very often:

expression: back and forth
spanish_sources: moving first in one direction and then in the opposite one:

expression: Off and on
spanish_sources: happening or existing only some of the time:

expression: lung
spanish_sources: either of the two organs in the chest with which people and some animals breathe:

expression: Hair
spanish_sources: The mass of thin, thread-like structures that grows on a person's head.

expression: Chin
spanish_sources: The part of a person's face below their mouth.

expression: Eyelid
spanish_sources: Either of the two pieces of skin that can close over each of your eyes.

expression: among
spanish_sources: in the middle of or surrounded by other things

expression: Pure and simple
spanish_sources: used after a noun to mean "and nothing else":

expression: From far and wide
spanish_sources: from a large number of places:

expression: Gum
spanish_sources: The pink tissue inside the mouth that covers the bones that hold the teeth.

expression: across
spanish_sources: from one side to the other side of something

expression: Nostril
spanish_sources: Either of the two openings at the end of the nose with which you smell and breathe.

expression: liver
spanish_sources: a large organ in the body that cleans the blood and produces bile, or this organ from an animal used as meat

expression: Mouth
spanish_sources: The opening on the face through which a person or animal eats and speaks.

expression: minus
spanish_sources: reduced by a stated number or amount

expression: Cheek
spanish_sources: The soft part of your face that is below your eye and between your mouth and ear.
